body_txt: People who are envious of others often wish to be like those they envy. As a result, it's not unknown for envious people to copy the targets of their envy. Said people typically do so either to become like the target of their envy or to gain or take something from the target of their envy. In some cases, the envier simply seeks to rival the target of their envy, while in other cases, the envious party seeks to surpass them. The nature of the mimicry involved depends on what exactly the envier envies. If someone is envious of someone's looks, they might try to copy or even surpass their looks via plastic surgery, makeup, or magic. If someone is envious of someone's popularity, the envier will copy whatever quality makes the envied person so popular. A homewrecker or a romantic rival might try to one-up their love rival by copying the traits they believe will make them look desirable to the target of their affections. The quality of the mimicry varies, though it rarely surpasses that of the original. Someone interested in popularity might focus on the superficial aspects of what made someone popular without any of the substance. Someone who mimics a skill will likely either focus on making themselves look better than the person they envy without actually improving their skills. Someone envious of someone's possessions might either get themselves into debt or buy cheap knockoffs because they can't afford to copy the other person. Compare Driven by Envy . See also I Just Want to Be You , for when someone wants to actually be the target of their envy. See also Irritation Is the Sincerest Form of Flattery for when the mimicked gets annoyed with the mimicker. An Attention Whore might do this to steal attention from someone else. Contrast Admiration-Based Mimicry .
